response_text:
# Git Branching Strategies: GitFlow vs Trunk-Based Development

## The Core Philosophy Difference

```
GitFlow: Multiple long-lived branches, structured releases
Trunk-Based: Everyone integrates to one branch frequently
```

---

## GitFlow

### Branch Structure
```
main/master          ─────●─────────────────────●──────────
                          ↑                     ↑
                        v1.0                  v2.0
hotfix               ────●──/
                        /
develop          ──●──●──────────────●──────────●──────────
                       \            /  \       /
feature/login    ────────●──●──●──/    \     /
                                        \   /
release/2.0      ────────────────────────●─/
```

### The Branches Explained

| Branch | Purpose | Lifespan |
|--------|---------|----------|
| `main` | Production-ready code only | Permanent |
| `develop` | Integration branch | Permanent |
| `feature/*` | New features | Until merged |
| `release/*` | Release preparation | Until released |
| `hotfix/*` | Production bug fixes | Until fixed |

### GitFlow in Practice
```bash
# Starting a feature
git checkout develop
git checkout -b feature/user-authentication

# Work, commit, work, commit...
git add .
git commit -m "Add JWT token validation"

# Finish feature - merge back to develop
git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff feature/user-authentication
git branch -d feature/user-authentication

# Starting a release
git checkout develop
git checkout -b release/2.1.0

# Only bug fixes on release branch
git commit -m "Fix login redirect bug"

# Finish release - merge to BOTH main AND develop
git checkout main
git merge --no-ff release/2.1.0
git tag -a v2.1.0

git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff release/2.1.0
git branch -d release/2.1.0

# Hotfix on production
git checkout main
git checkout -b hotfix/critical-security
